response: ISA, assume a major global retail company was creating a cross-border trade e-commerce marketplace integration platform. The platform aimed to streamline the process of online selling by small merchants in one country and online buying of those products across international borders by international shoppers, offering seamless integration of the company’s e-commerce platform in one region to multiple international marketplaces (for example, the company’s U.K., Canada, and Mexico marketplaces) and also to the international marketplaces of the company’s Strategic Partners like a major Chinese e-commerce company and a major Japanese e-commerce company. Top-tier U.K. companies already on the platform include a company specializing in wearable tech for sportspeople; a company based in Glasgow that sells home and garden products; a company that manufactures and sells sporting equipment from its headquarters in Wales; and a company based in Leicester that exports products to multiple countries worldwide. **Cross-Border Trade CBT E-commerce Marketplace Integration Platform Overview**: - *Purpose*: To develop a unified e-commerce platform that enables efficient B2B2C cross-border trade. - *Target Users*: Sellers and buyers in various international markets, including small businesses and large enterprises. - *Key Goals*: Enhance user experience, improve operational efficiency, and ensure compliance with international trade laws. The cross-border trade CBT e-commerce marketplace platform would also include Cross-border Fulfillment Services. The company’s Fulfillment Services (WFS) offers end-to-end fulfillment services for the company’s marketplace sellers. With one of the world’s largest supply chains, the company, and now building on it, was assumed to be uniquely positioned to provide the scale, quality, cost, and efficiency cross-border online marketplace sellers need to grow a successful business on any of the company’s global marketplaces. For example, sellers based in one country can take advantage of simplified onboarding, streamlined fulfillment, targeted promotions, integrated financial services, and other support to help them rapidly scale and grow in another region. Since sellers based in one country must be able to provide fast and reliable fulfillment delivered with duties prepaid (DDP), the WFS would be a substantial value-add to those sellers. *Fulfillment Model 1: Ship with Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace (Export Hub) Model:* The Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace Integration Platform is partnering with a major logistics provider to provide this logistics solution to the sellers. In this model, the logistics provider would take up the onus to clear the Customs Duty and complete the last-mile delivery. The cross-border e-commerce marketplace sellers
